rag_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks into the intended target component or implementation while preserving AADL architecture
Place component type contracts in component type scopes and implementation-level bindings in implementation scopes
Use valid component type scope for assumptions and guarantees referring to component ports
Preserve existing subcomponents and connections when adding implementation-level bindings
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
generic_rules: Preserve the original AADL architecture when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Ensure AGREE annex blocks are placed in valid scopes according to AADL structure
Use visible identifiers when referring to features in AGREE contracts
Avoid using implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Use `assume`, `guarantee`, `eq`, and `const` for AGREE contract elements
Use `assign` for implementation-level bindings in implementation annexes
anti_patterns: Do not place assumptions or guarantees referring to component ports in invalid scopes
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless properly represented
Avoid using implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
irrelevant_topics: AGREE, annex, assume, guarantee, assign, validator, or repair
